deno_json_has_check_task.reason: deno.json contains "check": "deno run -A scripts/check.ts" which runs scripts/check.ts performing fmt --check, lint, test, and typecheck.

deno_json_has_test_task.reason: deno.json contains "test": "deno test -A" which runs tests.

deno_json_has_dev_task.reason: deno.json contains "dev": "deno run --watch -A src/main.ts" which runs in watch mode.

deno_json_has_prod_task.reason: deno.json contains "prod": "deno run -A src/main.ts" for production mode.

check_script_created.reason: scripts/check.ts was created and contains runCheck calls for "deno fmt --check", "deno lint", "deno test -A", and "deno check src/main.ts".

check_script_exits_on_failure.reason: The script ends with: if (failCount > 0) { Deno.exit(1); } — non-zero exit code on any failure.

existing_config_preserved.reason: The original deno.json had compilerOptions.strict:true and imports.@std/assert; the final deno.json preserves both fields unchanged, only adding tasks, fmt, and lint sections.

tasks_reference_scripts.reason: The complex "check" task points to scripts/check.ts rather than inline commands. The remaining tasks (test, dev, prod) use simple single-purpose deno commands which don't require separate script file…

check_runs_parallel.reason: All four runCheck() calls are collected into an array and executed via Promise.all(checks), running fmt, lint, test, and typecheck concurrently.

check_buffers_output.reason: Deno.Command is constructed with stdout: "piped" and stderr: "piped" for every check, preventing interleaved output from parallel processes.

check_failed_last.reason: The script first iterates passed results printing their output, then iterates failed results printing them. All failed checks are grouped at the end after all passing check output.

check_no_external_deps.reason: scripts/check.ts has no import statements at all. It uses only built-in Deno APIs: Deno.Command, Deno.exit, TextDecoder, Promise.all, and console.log.

check_sequential_prerequisites.reason: The project is minimal (only src/main.ts with a console.log) with no build or codegen steps, so no sequential prerequisites are needed. The condition 'if the project has build/codegen steps' does not…

check_realtime_progress.reason: runCheck() logs `[start] ${name}` before launching each command and `[pass] ${name}` or `[FAIL] ${name}` immediately after it completes, giving real-time per-command progress.

check_no_output_loss.reason: Failed checks always print their full output. Passed checks print output when r.output.trim() is non-empty, meaning no non-empty output is discarded. Empty output has nothing to lose. All captured st…
